heading: Expanding access to forensics for victims of interpersonal violence
label: 280g–4c Expanding access to forensics for victims of interpersonal violence
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Violence Against Women Act Reauthorization Act of 2022, and also as part of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2022, and not as part of the Public Health Service Act which comprises this chapter.

Effective DateSection not effective until Oct. 1 of the first fiscal year beginning after , see , set out as a note under , Commerce and Trade.Mar. 15, 2022section 4(a) of div. W of Pub. L. 117–103section 6851 of Title 15

DefinitionsFor definition of “law enforcement” and “State” as used in this section, see , Crime Control and Law Enforcement, as made applicable by , which is set out as a note under .section 12291 of Title 34section 2(b) of div. W of Pub. L. 117–103section 12291 of Title 34

children.heading: Community health aide; community health practitioner
children.label: 1 Community health aide; community health practitioner
children.text: The terms “community health aide” and “community health practitioner” have the meanings given such terms for purposes of section 1616 of title 25.l

children.heading: Health care provider
children.label: 2 Health care provider
children.text: The term “health care provider” has the meaning given such term by the Secretary, and includes registered nurses, nurse practitioners, nurse midwives, clinical nurse specialists, physician assistants, and physicians.

children.heading: Indian tribe; Tribal organization
children.label: 3 Indian tribe; Tribal organization
children.text: The terms “Indian Tribe” and “Tribal organization” shall have the meanings given such terms in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 4 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given such term in .section 1001 of title 20

children.heading: Interpersonal violence
children.label: 5 Interpersonal violence
children.text: The term “interpersonal violence” means any form of violence that is emotional and trauma-inducing for victims, families of victims, perpetrators, and communities.

children.heading: Native Hawaiian organization
children.label: 6 Native Hawaiian organization
children.text: The term “Native Hawaiian organization” has the meaning given such term in .section 11711 of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 7 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Trauma-informed care
children.label: 8 Trauma-informed care
children.text: The term “trauma-informed care” means care received by trauma survivors that is culturally competent in accordance with professional standards of practice and accounting for patients’ experiences and preferences in order to eliminate or mitigate triggers that may cause re-traumatization of the patient.

children.heading: Urban Indian organization
children.label: 9 Urban Indian organization
children.text: The term “Urban Indian organization” has the meaning given such term in .section 1603 of title 25

children.heading: Demonstration grants for comprehensive forensic training
children.label: b Demonstration grants for comprehensive forensic training

children.heading: Establishment of program
children.label: 1 Establishment of program
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a demonstration program to award grants to eligible entities for the clinical training of health care providers to provide generalist forensic services and trauma-informed care to survivors of interpersonal violence of all ages.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the demonstration program under this subsection is to develop training and curriculum to provide health care providers with the skills to support the provision of forensic assessment and trauma-informed care to individuals, families, and communities that have experienced violence or trauma and to be available to collaborate with members of an inter-professional forensic team.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 3 Term
children.text: Grants under this subsection shall be for a term of 5 years.

children.label: A
children.text: be an institute of higher education, including a minority serving institution as described in ; andsection 1067q of title 20

children.label: B
children.text: submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Grant amount
children.label: 5 Grant amount
children.text: Each grant awarded under this subsection shall be in an amount that does not exceed $400,000 per year. A grant recipient may carry over funds from one fiscal year to the next without obtaining approval from the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $5,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.heading: Set-aside
children.label: B Set-aside
children.text: Of the amount appropriated under this paragraph for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall reserve 10 percent for purposes of making grants to support training and curricula that addresses the unique needs of Indian Tribes, Tribal organizations, Urban Indian organizations, and Native Hawaiian organizations. Amounts so reserved may be used to support training, referrals, and the delivery of emergency first aid, culturally competent support, and forensic evidence collection training.

children.heading: Technical assistance grants and learning collectives
children.label: c Technical assistance grants and learning collectives

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a State and Tribal forensic provider technical resource center to provide technical assistance and support collaboration and best practices for health care providers, community health aides, and community health practitioners to improve the quality of, and increase access to, forensic services for all survivors of interpersonal violence. The Secretary may enter into contracts with national experts for purposes of carrying out this subsection.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection, $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

access to generalist medical forensic services, evidence collection, and documentation that aids in meeting the needs of health care patients and improves future law enforcement investigation and prosecution; and

data for research to support the response to and prevention of interpersonal violence, improved ability of health care providers to adequately respond to patients who exhibit signs of victimization, and address the unique needs of Tribal communities.
